campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including money in investment accounts and the treasurer's report. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, including walking, biking, and public transit.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment account balances and transactions
Treasurer's financial report for July
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan - improving walking, biking, and transit options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could speak about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipping the usual first-reading step to speed up the process. The ordinance will be voted on at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and expedited for faster approval
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading of ordinance waived to streamline process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda focused mainly on administrative housekeeping and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved multiple sets of meeting minutes from March 2026 sessions
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Closed session discussions (specific topics not detailed in agenda)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review progress on programs helping kids and teens in the city, including youth empowerment initiatives and summer safety programs. They also discussed updates on code enforcement activities and plans to improve the city's library facilities and services for visitors.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and mentoring programs for teens
Safe Summer Initiative Grant funding for youth activities
Code Enforcement annual report on violations and compliance
Library facility improvements and customer service updates

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included both in-person and online participation options. Residents who wanted to speak during the meeting could join via Zoom or phone to give public comments on agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for online participants
Hand-raising feature for speakers to queue up during discussion items

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the official minutes from their previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission meeting and flag ceremony
Personal stories shared by Commissioners 5, 6, and 7
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community initiatives including a food-related program called Budget and Bites, hear from a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and present information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved meeting minutes from their previous March meeting and opened the floor for residents to speak about any local issues.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City of Cupertino summer jobs for youth
Public comment period for residents
Approval of March 11 meeting minutes

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council is holding their April 7, 2026 meeting using both in-person and online options so residents can participate however works best for them. The council is making it easy for people to join remotely via Zoom or phone if they can't attend in person, and residents can submit written comments before the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format - attend in person or join online via Zoom
Remote participation options via phone (1-669-900-6833)
Written public comment submission through eComment tab on city website
Meeting details and agenda available on city council page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to discuss updates to the city's federal funding plans for 2025-2026, review requests from individual council members for new projects or initiatives, and consider changing the rules for filling council vacancies. The council also approved meeting minutes and previewed items coming up at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Federal funding plan updates for housing and community development (2025-2030)
Changes to how council vacancies are filled
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Approval of meeting minutes and calendar scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the city's gas utility budget and rates for the next fiscal year. The council reviewed financial plans for the gas utility and voted on potential changes to residential and commercial gas service rates.
keyTopics: Gas utility budget approval for fiscal year 2027
Rate schedule changes for residential gas customers
Rate adjustments for commercial and large commercial gas service
Transfer of funds between utility reserves and the general city budget
